structured_content.text:
View
                        email in browser Iger’s P.E. Option, Greeny’s ESPN Memoirs, The NFL-Amazon Tryst Welcome back to The Daily Courant, your afternoon dispatch containing Puck’s best new reporting. First up today, Dylan Byers reveals the fresh tremors inside Hudson Yards as Mark Thompson finally unveils concrete plans for CNN’s political coverage during Trump 2.0—one week after the network averaged its lowest total day audience in a quarter of a century . But is MAGA-world anthropology actually a winning programming strategy? Plus, below the fold: Marion Maneker chronicles the tug-of-war over Artnet between the controlling Neuendorf family and a major shareholder. Bill Cohan makes the case for WBD, Disney, and NBCU going the P.E. route as their streaming businesses finally turn a corner and they look to offload terminally declining TV assets. On The Varsity , John Ourand huddles up with ESPN’s Mike Greenberg to revisit his nearly three decades at the network and the evolving market for on-air talent. And on The Powers That Be , Jon Kelly reunites with Peter Hamby to discuss the latest dramas underpinning the media industry: fallout from the NFL-Amazon romance, the plummeting ratings for CNN and MSNBC, and more. The Mark-est Hour DYLAN BYERS A gloomy company-wide meeting revealed Mark Thompson’s CNN is still caught between the linear past and the digital future, and nonpolitical news versus 24-hour Trump TV. Insiders wonder who’s paying attention to the content while others wonder if a more sinister fate beckons. A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR Developed in partnership with a coalition of leading community groups, Capital One’s five-year community benefits plan includes significant financial and programmatic commitments through community development, Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs), philanthropy and pro bono, consumer card and auto lending, small business and supplier diversity, and bank access. These commitments will aim to expand economic opportunity for underserved consumers, including those in low- and moderate-income (LMI) neighborhoods, rural areas, and communities of color. It will also support increased access to best-in-class products and services for unbanked or underbanked consumers as well as consumers across the credit spectrum and expand access to capital and opportunity. Important information about our proposed acquisition at CapitalOneDiscover.com Keeping Up With the Neuendorfs MARION MANEKER A major Artnet shareholder is crying foul over the Neuendorf family’s abrupt cancellation of this week’s annual meeting, delaying an inevitable boardroom showdown. Cable Prepares for the Value Extraction Era WILLIAM D. COHAN With the once-unprofitable streaming business finally turning a corner, NBCU, Disney, and WBD are all openly telegraphing what seems increasingly inevitable: spinning off or selling declining TV assets while there’s still value to extract. Enter private equity, an industry that knows a thing or two about value extraction… Getting Up With Greeny JOHN OURAND John is joined by one of ESPN’s brightest stars, Mike “Greeny” Greenberg. In a wide-ranging conversation, the duo dive into Greeny’s remarkable 28-year (and counting) career at the network, the market for on-air talent and how it’s changed over the years, how his show ‘Get Up’ became a cornerstone of ESPN’s morning programming, and much more. Media Monday: NFL’s Amazon Romance & TV’s Trump-Induced Nosedive JON KELLY & PETER HAMBY Jon Kelly reunites with Peter to discuss the latest dramas underpinning the media industry: why TV execs believe the traditional networks are being sidelined in the NFL-Amazon partnership, the plummeting ratings of CNN and MSNBC in the wake of Trump’s victory, and much, much more. Need help? Review our FAQs

                                    us for assistance. For brand partnerships, email ads@puck.news . To stop receiving this newsletter and/or manage all your email preferences, click here . Puck is published by Heat Media LLC. 227 W 17th St New York, NY 10011.
